items.title: Last call before car prices climb: Automakers set for another round of hikes in April
items.summary: After January hikes by Mercedes-Benz, Bmw and Hyundai, Tata Motors PV will raise prices next week while more automakers review increases, driven by higher aluminium and steel prices, a weaker rupee and state tax revisions.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki to infuse ₹10,189 crore in Gujarat plant for capacity expansion
items.summary: The Maruti Suzuki Board approved the new infusion of capital that includes investment for the first phase of car manufacturing plant having an annual manufacturing capacity of 250,000 units and some common infrastructure and facilities for future plants

items.title: Suzuki 2W Sales Breakup Feb 2026 – Access, Burgman, Avenis, Gixxer, Hayabusa
items.summary: Suzuki Motorcycle India recorded total domestic sales of 1,01,071 units in February 2026, registering a strong 38.05% YoY growth The post Suzuki 2W Sales Breakup Feb 2026 – Access, Burgman, Avenis, Gixxer, Hayabusa appeared first on RushLane .

items.title: Helmet manufacturers flag raw material price surge amid Gulf War disruptions
items.summary: The surge is attributed to disruptions in global petrochemical supply chains, higher freight costs, and volatility in crude oil markets, with impact being felt across manufacturing sectors, including the two-wheeler safety equipment segment.

items.title: ​Volkswagen's software partnership with Rivian clears investment hurdle
items.summary: The joint ​venture aims to develop a ⁠software platform ‌to underpin a model revamp ​across ​Volkswagen's core brand as well as ⁠its US pickup truck business Scout and ​premium subsidiary Audi.

items.title: Supplier ZF gives downbeat revenue outlook over weak commercial vehicle sector
items.summary: In 2025, ZF's revenue declined roughly 6 per cent, to 38.8 billion euros ($44.5 billion), ​from 41.4 billion the previous ⁠year, reflecting divestitures ‌and foreign exchange effects.

items.title: Mahindra flags ₹5,000 crore penalty risk under CAFE III, seeks EV-friendly policy tweaks
items.summary: Mahindra & Mahindra has cautioned that proposed CAFE III norms could lead to annual penalties of up to ₹5,000 crore, urging the government to revisit emission targets, EV treatment and credit mechanisms

items.title: GM, LG retool Tennessee battery plant for energy storage batteries, recall laid-off workers
items.summary: LG has been transitioning some of its EV battery capacity to energy storage batteries, and some competitors, including SK On, are doing the same after policy shifts under US President Donald Trump hurt EV demand.

items.title: Tata Motors PV shares slide 5% as JLR halts UK production
items.summary: The disruption, though short-term, has raised concerns over supply chain challenges and near-term earnings visibility.

items.title: Exide Industries infuses ₹450 crore into EV battery arm Exide Energy Solutions
items.summary: Exide Industries has invested ₹450 crore in its wholly owned subsidiary Exide Energy Solutions through a rights issue to fund its upcoming lithium-ion cell manufacturing plant in Bengaluru

items.title: RACL Geartech Flags Short-Term Production Impact Due to Gas Supply Constraints
items.summary: RACL Geartech Limited has indicated that intermittent shortages in industrial gas supplies have affected its operations during the current month, as spillover effects from the ongoing Iran–Israel conflict tighten fuel availability. In a regulatory communication, the company said the availability of LPG, PNG and CNG—key inputs for its manufacturing processes as well as those of its suppliers—has been inconsistent,...

items.title: Kirloskar Ferrous Restores Solapur Plant Operations After LPG Disruption
items.summary: Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Limited has resumed partial operations at its Solapur manufacturing facility after a brief stoppage caused by a shortage of Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG). In a regulatory filing to BSE Limited dated 21 March 2026, the company stated that one of two High Pressure Moulding Lines (HPMLs) at the plant was back in operation, with an alternate fuel substituted for LPG.
